steps.title: Match the acute angles
steps.content: In triangle $ABC$, the given acute angle is $32^{\circ}$, so the other acute angle is $58^{\circ}$. Triangle $DEF$ also has a $58^{\circ}$ acute angle, so the right triangles are similar.

steps.title: Match corresponding sides
steps.content: The ratio $\frac{bc}{ab}$ means $\frac{BC}{AB}$. In triangle $ABC$, that is the leg opposite $32^{\circ}$ over the hypotenuse. In triangle $DEF$, the matching ratio is $\frac{DF}{DE}$, which is written as $df/de$. The answer is B.
